title: Modules, Packages, and Dependency Boundaries · Spiral Review
summary: Split the status coordinator into explicit browser modules and verify clean-environment loading and dependency behavior. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a modular status application with a dependency record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.
Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
Use dynamic import for conditional capability or measured loading needs with complete error states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
